chapter_title.ar: بَابُ كَفَّارَاتِ مَا أَصَابَ الْمُحْرِمُ مِنَ الْوَحْشِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 109 – The expiations on what the one in Ihraam attains from the wild animals

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/373)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/373)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَتَلَ ثَعْلَباً قَالَ عَلَيْهِ دَمٌ قُلْتُ فَأَرْنَباً قَالَ مِثْلُ مَا عَلَى الثَّعْلَبِ .

en.hubeali: Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about a man who kills a fox. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Upon him is blood (of a sacrificial animal)’. I said, ‘So (what about) a rabbit?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Similar to what is (Obligated) upon the fox’.
en.sarwar: 7. Sahl ibn Ziyad has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn abu Hamzah from abu Basir who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who kills a fox. He (the Imam) said, ‘He owes one animal offering.’ I asked about his killing a rabbit. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is like what is for a fox.’”
